segments.text: After all this had happened, God spoke to Abram in a vision, telling him,
Don't be afraid, Abram! I am your protector, and your truly great reward!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But Abram replied,
Lord God, what good is whatever you give me? I don't have any children, and the heir to all that I have is Eliezer of Damascus.
segments.speaker: Abram

segments.text: Abram went on to complain,
Look! You haven't given me any children, so a servant from my household has to be my heir!
segments.speaker: Abram

segments.text: But then the Lord told him,
This man won't be your heir. Your heir will be your very own son.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: The Lord took Abram outside and said to him,
Look up at the sky. See if you can count the stars! That's how many descendants you will have!
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Abram trusted what the Lord said, and so the Lord counted Abram as being in a right relationship with him.
The Lord also told him,
I am the Lord, who led you from Ur of the Chaldeans to give you this land for you to own.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But Lord God, how can I be certain that I will own it?
segments.speaker: Abram

segments.text: Abram asked.
The Lord told him,
Bring me a cow, a goat, and a ram, all of them three years old, together with a dove and a young pigeon.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: So Abram took and killed the three animals. Then he cut them in half, and placed each half opposite the other. However, he didn't cut the birds in half.
When vultures flew down on the carcasses, Abram frightened them off.
As the sun went down, a deep sleep came over Abram, and at the same time a dense and terrifying darkness fell on him.
The Lord explained to Abram,
You can be absolutely sure that your descendants will be strangers in a foreign land, where they will be slaves and mistreated for 400 years.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: However, I will punish the nation that makes them slaves, and later on they will leave, taking many valuable possessions with them.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But as for you, you will die in peace and be buried, having lived a good long life.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Four generations later your descendants will come back here to live, because right now the sins of the Amorites have not reached their full extent.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: After the sun set and it grew dark, suddenly a smoking furnace and a flaming torch appeared and passed between the halves of the animal carcasses.
This is how the Lord made an agreement with Abram that day and promised him,
I'm giving this land to your descendants. It extends from the Wadi of Egypt[^65] to the great Euphrates River,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: and includes the territory of the Kenites, Kenizzites, Kadmonites,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Hittites, Perizzites, Rephaites,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Amorites, Canaanites, Girgashites, and Jebusites.
segments.speaker: the Lord
